free_text: Charge distribution of light mass fission products in
the 232U(nth, f), 238Pu(nth, f) and 244Cm(SF) reactions
For neutron induced fission, 100 uL nitrate solution
of 232U (100 ug/ml) and 238Pu (50 ug/ml) were sealed
in different polypropylene tubes of about 3-cm long
and 3-mm inner diameter.
Samples were irradiated for 3 to 60 min in the reactor
APSARA at a flux of 1.2 X 10**12 n/cm2/sec.
For spontaneous fission of 244Cm, 96 ug of curium was
electrodeposited on a platinum planchet and collected
on 75 um thick different Lexan catcher.
105 cm3 detector used for the neutron
irradiation of 232U and 238Pu with relative efficiency
of 20%
120 cm3 detector used for the neutron irradiation of
244Cm with relative efficiency of 30%.
The resolution of the detector systems were 2.0 keV and
1.8 keV at 1332.5 keV for the 105 cm3 and 120 cm3
detectors, respectively.
The dead time of detector always kept less than 10%.
The error in the FCY of the light mass
fission products are determined from the statistical
uncertainties in cumulative yields based on replicate
measurements using the parent-daughter genetic relation
VT
